title: Explain the difference between == and equals() in Java
description: Describe when to use == versus equals() method for comparing objects in Java, including primitives and reference types.

title: What is the difference between String, StringBuilder, and StringBuffer?
description: Compare and contrast String, StringBuilder, and StringBuffer classes in terms of mutability, thread-safety, and performance.

title: Explain method overloading vs method overriding
description: Describe the differences between method overloading and method overriding with examples of when to use each.

title: What are the access modifiers in Java?
description: List and explain all access modifiers in Java (public, private, protected, default) and their visibility scope.

title: Explain the final keyword in Java
description: Describe how the final keyword works when applied to variables, methods, and classes.

title: What is autoboxing and unboxing?
description: Explain the automatic conversion between primitive types and their corresponding wrapper classes in Java.

title: Difference between ArrayList and LinkedList
description: Compare ArrayList and LinkedList in terms of internal structure, performance characteristics, and use cases.

title: What is the purpose of the static keyword?
description: Explain how static works for variables, methods, blocks, and nested classes in Java.

title: Explain checked vs unchecked exceptions
description: Describe the difference between checked and unchecked exceptions, with examples of each type.

title: What is the difference between abstract class and interface?
description: Compare abstract classes and interfaces in Java, including when to use each and their key differences.

title: Implement a custom immutable class
description: Design and implement a fully immutable class in Java following best practices, including handling mutable fields.

title: Explain the contract between equals() and hashCode()
description: Describe the relationship between equals() and hashCode() methods and why they must be overridden together.

title: How does HashMap work internally?
description: Explain the internal implementation of HashMap including hashing, collision handling, and resizing mechanism.

title: Implement a singleton pattern with thread safety
description: Create a thread-safe singleton implementation using different approaches (eager, lazy, double-checked locking, enum).

title: Explain Java memory model and garbage collection
description: Describe the Java memory structure (heap, stack, metaspace) and how garbage collection works.

title: What are generics and type erasure?
description: Explain Java generics, type parameters, bounded types, and the concept of type erasure at runtime.

title: Implement a custom exception hierarchy
description: Design a custom exception hierarchy for a specific domain with proper use of checked and unchecked exceptions.

title: Explain the Comparable vs Comparator interfaces
description: Compare Comparable and Comparator interfaces, when to use each, and implement custom sorting logic.

title: What is reflection and when should you use it?
description: Explain Java reflection API, its capabilities, use cases, and performance implications.

title: Implement a deep copy mechanism
description: Create a mechanism to perform deep copy of objects including nested objects and collections.

title: Design a custom class loader
description: Implement a custom ClassLoader that can load classes from non-standard sources with proper delegation.

title: Implement a thread-safe LRU cache
description: Design and implement a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache with O(1) operations that is thread-safe.

title: Explain JVM internals and bytecode
description: Describe how the JVM executes bytecode, including class loading, verification, JIT compilation, and optimization.

title: Implement a custom annotation processor
description: Create a compile-time annotation processor that generates code or validates annotations.

title: Design a type-safe heterogeneous container
description: Implement a container that can hold different types safely using generics and type tokens.

title: Explain weak, soft, and phantom references
description: Describe the different types of references in Java and their use cases for memory-sensitive caching.

title: Implement a custom serialization mechanism
description: Create a custom serialization framework that handles circular references and version compatibility.

title: Design a dynamic proxy with method interception
description: Implement a dynamic proxy using Java's Proxy class or CGLIB that intercepts method calls for AOP-like behavior.

title: Explain happens-before relationship and memory visibility
description: Describe the Java Memory Model's happens-before guarantees and how they ensure memory visibility across threads.

title: Implement a custom concurrent collection
description: Design a thread-safe collection with fine-grained locking or lock-free algorithms for high concurrency.
